mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate directly eases discount conditions and supports equity multiples, so a month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already above 4.5%, additional tightening raises the probability of equity de-rating and near-term drawdowns, so the signal becomes negative for S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold FEDFUNDS readings are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average 5d return after threshold breaches is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening income growth and deteriorating earnings expectations, so a 3-month increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rises above the trigger and the subsequent 20d SPX return is positive more often than negative, or if the trigger-conditioned mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth and unemployment together capture labor-market momentum: when PAYEMS keeps rising while UNRATE is flat or falling, earnings risk is lower and S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition PAYEMS up and UNRATE down does not outperform the unconditional 60d return, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Improving retail sales indicate stronger final demand and revenue momentum, so a rising RSAFS trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in most triggered months, or if the signal's rolling Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production reflects weakening output and margin pressure, so a negative 2-month change in INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive-month INDPRO declines are not associated with below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the signal flips sign in more than one-third of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and sales together measure cyclical household demand; when HOUST rises while RSAFS also improves, the combination signals broad demand acceleration that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-RSAFS improvement does not beat either proxy alone, or if positive 20d returns do not occur more often than negative returns under the combined condition.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary regime, lower CPI inflation with a steady policy rate implies falling real rates and easier financial conditions, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation slows for 3 months while FEDFUNDS is unchanged
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus unchanged policy does not produce above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal is outperformed by a simpler single-series CPI or FEDFUNDS rule.
